title: Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)
content:

The '''''Star Wars: X-Wing''''' computer game is the first [[LucasArts]] PC game set in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe. Benefiting from the renewed interest in ''Star Wars'' sparked by [[Timothy Zahn]]'s [[Star Wars: The Thrawn Trilogy|''The Thrawn Trilogy'']] and building on the popularity of flight simulation software ([[Lawrence Holland]]'s three previous World War II air combat games had all been huge successes for LucasArts), ''X-Wing'' achieved what few subsequent ''Star Wars'' games could by being critically acclaimed, embraced by fans and a financial success. Players assumed the role of [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], a young pilot in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], and were given the opportunity to pilot the eponymous [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]], as well as the [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]], and the [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wing]], in a number of key battles leading up to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].

==Publisher's summary==
''The Old Republic is gone. The Senate has been abolished. The Jedi Knights have been exterminated. Now the Emperor seeks to crush the last remaining opposition. Against the overwhelming might of the Empire stands a small but growing Rebel Alliance. The scattered resistance groups are uniting, every spacecraft marshaled and every being recruited. What is needed most ''now'' are starfighter pilots. Will you join their struggle to end this tyranny? Will ''you'' become a hero of the Rebellion?''<ref name="XW">''Star Wars: X-Wing''</ref>

==Plot summary==
===Introduction===
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=Star Wars: X-Wing
|text=It is a period of Civil War.
Using fear and intimidation,
the Empire seeks to impose a
New Order on the galaxy. Only
the Rebel Alliance stands in
the way of their evil plans.

Not yet willing to confront the
Empire directly, the Rebels are
marshalling their forces in
secrecy. Even now a group of
their prized Calamari Cruisers is
being intercepted by a squadron
of Imperial Star Destroyers.

The Rebel Fleet is outnumbered
but, they have a surprise…
the X-Wing Starfighter!}}
A [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] of Rebel [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruisers]] is [[Battle of Turkana/Legends|attacked]] by an [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] patrol near the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Turkana/Legends|Turkana]]. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]], in command of the Rebel fleet, orders the launch of the Alliance's new [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing]], leading to a surprise Rebel victory.<ref name="XW" />

===Tour of Duty I: A New Ally===
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=Tour of Duty I
A NEW ALLY
|text=Shocked by Imperial losses
at Turkana at the hands of
the Rebels, the Emperor
has ordered a new campaign
to crush the Alliance.

Meanwhile, the Rebel High
Command, encouraged by the
X-wing's success against the
Empire has deployed a new
starfighter, the A-wing.

It is feared, though, that
Emperor Palpatine has his
own surprises.}}
In retaliation for the loss at Turkana, [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine orders a major offensive named [[Operation Strike Fear]]. During a Rebel [[Battle in the Dellalt system|hit and fade strike]] against an Imperial convoy, the crew of the Imperial [[CR90 corvette/Legends|corvette]] ''[[Bixby]]'' defect to the Alliance. [[Nogdra|The captain of the ''Bixby'']] alerts the Rebels to Imperial forces, led by the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Invincible (Strike Fear)|''Invincible'']], massing in the [[Dellalt system/Legends|Dellalt system]] in preparation for the upcoming campaign.<ref name="XW" />

Operation Strike Fear soon begins with an [[Battle of Brigia (Galactic Civil War)|assault]] on the [[Brigia Rebel base|Rebel base]] on [[Brigia/Legends|Brigia]]. Rebel starfighters provide cover for the evacuation of the base and the [[Skirmish near Brigia|transfer]] of wounded personnel to the [[Hospital ship/Legends|medical frigate]] [[Redemption/Legends|''Redemption'']]. The Empire follows up on the victory at Brigia with an [[Battle of Orion IV|assault]] on the [[Orion IV Rebel base|Rebel base]] on [[Orion IV]], leaving the facility in ruins and leading to the capture of many Rebels.<ref name="XW" />

The Empire next sets its sights on [[Rudrig/Legends|Rudrig]], but the Rebels move quickly to [[Raid on the Juno convoy|ambush]] a resupply convoy bound for the ''Invincible'', delaying the next phase of Operation Strike Fear and giving the Alliance time to [[Raid in the Rudrig system|secure]] a supply of desperately needed [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droids]]. Unknown to the Rebels, the R2 [[Droid/Legends|droids]] have been programmed to take control of any X-wings they are put into and deliver them to the Empire. Three X-wings are stolen by the R2s and the Rebels launch a [[Mission to recover stolen X-wings|mission]] to recover them and their pilots before they fall into Imperial hands.<ref name="XW" />

For some time, the Rebel Alliance has been attempting to recruit the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]] to their cause. When the Alliance learns that Sullustan [[Technician/Legends|technical staff]] have been kidnapped by Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], they launch a [[Rescue of Sullustan Tech Staff|rescue]] operation. The success of the mission leads the Sullustan leadership to agree to a [[Diplomatic Summit at Sullust|diplomatic summit]] near [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]]. During the meeting, Imperial forces capture the [[Sian Tevv/Legends|Sullustan leader]] on the planet's surface. The Rebels mount a [[Rescue of Sian Tevv|rescue mission]], which results in the Sullustans joining the Alliance.<ref name="XW" />

Working with [[General/Legends|General]] [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]], Sullustan technicians devise a plan to stop Operation Strike Fear by destroying the ''Invincible''. To this end, the Alliance launches an mission to [[Capture of Cygnus Staff|capture]] design staff from [[Cygnus Spaceworks/Legends|Cygnus Spaceworks]] during performance evaluations of their latest spacecraft. During a subsequent [[Capture of the Gafra|operation]], the Rebels steal an Imperial explosive device. The captive Cygnus staff modify a Rebel [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] to pose as an Imperial ship. Rebel agents use the shuttle to deliver the explosive device to the ''Invincible'', which detonates shortly after they depart, destroying the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]].<ref name="XW" />

====Missions====
#[[Battle in the Dellalt system|Destroy Imperial Convoy]]
#[[Mission in the Dellalt system|ID Reconnaissance Mission]]
#[[Battle of Brigia (Galactic Civil War)|Fly Point During Evacuation]]
#[[Skirmish near Brigia|Protect Medical Frigate]]
#[[Raid on the Juno convoy|Ambush Imperial Supply Convoy]]
#[[Raid in the Rudrig system|Raid for R2 Units]]
#[[Mission to recover stolen X-wings|Recover Stolen X-wings]]
#[[Rescue of Sullustan Tech Staff|Rescue Sullustan Tech Staff]]
#[[Diplomatic Summit at Sullust]]
#[[Rescue of Sian Tevv|Rescue Sullustan Leader]]
#[[Capture of Cygnus Staff|Capture Staff from Cygnus]]
#[[Capture of the Gafra|Recover Explosive Warhead]]

===Tour of Duty II: The Great Search===
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=Tour of Duty II
THE GREAT SEARCH
|text=Fresh from their stunning
success against Operation
Strike Fear, the Alliance has
found renewed hope in their
struggle against the Empire.

Now word comes of an even
greater threat, a vast secret
Imperial military project of such
awesome destructive power that
it could crush the Alliance!

Alarmed by these rumors, Rebel
scouts are desperately seeking
any news of this project.}}
When two Rebel pilots escape from the [[Prison/Legends|prison]] world [[Stars' End/Legends|Stars' End]] aboard a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] bound for [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], the Alliance mounts a [[Rescue from Stars' End|mission]] to recover them before the Empire. The pilots alert the alliance to a freighter carrying [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] slaves to a secret Imperial military construction project. The Rebels again launch a [[Rescue of enslaved Wookiees|rescue operation]] in the hopes of learning more about the project. Documents recovered from the freighter carrying the Wookiees lead the Alliance to an Imperial weapons convoy bound for the construction site. [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] consider it important to delay the project while they [[Operation Skyhook|attempt to learn more]], and starfighters are [[Battle of Orron III|dispatched]] to destroy the convoy as it passes by [[Orron III]].<ref name="XW" />

[[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] devises a plan to learn more by tapping into an Imperial communications network. When intelligence learns that a [[Phoenix (BFF-1 bulk freighter)|freighter]] carrying communication [[Satellite/Legends|satellites]] has been stolen from an Imperial base, they move quickly to [[Ambush of the Phoenix|capture]] the freighter. The captured freighter comes under [[Attack on the Phoenix|attack]] from the Star Destroyer [[Intrepid (Imperial I-class)|''Intrepid'']], but makes it safely to the cruiser ''[[Maximus]]''.<ref name="XW" />

With the satellites secured, they are [[Ambush in the Cron Drift|transferred]] to the corvette ''[[Jeffrey]]'' near a [[AX-235|Rebel listening post]] in the [[Cron Drift/Legends|Cron Drift]], and are deployed in place of several Imperial satellites. The satellites allow the listening post to intercept [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] for the the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], an Imperial [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] with a [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] capable of destroying a planet. Due to the importance of the plans, it is decided to send them via courier rather than risk transmission. However, the X-wing carrying the plans breaks down not far from the Cron Drift and the Rebels dispatched ships to [[Rescue of Biggs Darklighter|repair]] it.<ref name="XW" />

While [[Circarpous sector/Legends|Circarpous]] [[Sector Command (Alliance)/Legends|Sector Command]] work on a plan to get the plans to Alliance High Command, the Rebels learn that the ''Intrepid'' was damaged in a collision with a [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] and is in need of repairs. The Alliance launches an [[Battle of the Circarpous system (Galactic Civil War)|operation]] to destroy the ''Intrepid''. The first phase is to intercept and destroy freighters carrying replacement [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] parts to the stricken vessel. With the repairs prevented, the Rebels next moved to eliminate the Star Destroyer's protective escort, before finally moving againt the ''Intrepid'' itself.<ref name="XW" />

With the threat of the ''Intrepid'' removed, the Death Star plans are [[Death Star plans delivery|delivered]] to [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] aboard the corvette [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']] for delivery to [[Chief of State (Alliance)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mom Mothma]]. Organa plans to transfer to the cruiser [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']], but is forced to flee by the arrival of the Star Destroyer [[Immortal/Legends|''Immortal'']]. The Empire, having learned of Organa's cargo, pursues the fleeing corvette in an attempt to [[Assault on the Tantive IV|recover]] the plans, and the Alliance sends urgent reinforcements to help ''Tantive IV'' escape to safety.<ref name="XW" />

====Missions====
#[[Rescue from Stars' End]]
#[[Rescue of enslaved Wookiees|Rescue Wookiee Slaves]]
#[[Battle of Orron III|Attack Weapons Convoy]]
#[[Ambush of the Phoenix|Capture Stolen Freighter]]
#[[Attack on the Phoenix|Protect Captured Satellites]]
#[[Ambush in the Cron Drift]]
#[[Rescue of Biggs Darklighter|Protect a disabled X-wing]]
#[[Battle of the Circarpous system (Galactic Civil War)#First phase|Stop Hyperdrive Replacement]]
#[[Battle of the Circarpous system (Galactic Civil War)#Second phase|Take Out Intrepid's Escort]]
#[[Battle of the Circarpous system (Galactic Civil War)#Third phase|Destroy the ''Intrepid'']]
#[[Death Star plans delivery|Deliver Plans to Princess Leia]]
#[[Assault on the Tantive IV|Protect Princess Leia]]

===Tour of Duty III: The Gathering Storm===
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=Tour of Duty III
THE GATHERING STORM
|text=Having discovered the
existence of the DEATH STAR
project, the Rebel Alliance
prepares for its greatest
challenge. With the technical
plans in the hands of Princess
Leia, the Alliance gathers its
forces to destroy the Empire's
ultimate weapon.

The DEATH STAR, nearing
completion at a secret Imperial
base, is being prepared for its
reign of terror.}}

{{Expandsect}}
''The Gathering Storm'': describes the Rebels' route to the final [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|trench run]] and the Death Star destruction. This Tour has two more missions, which occur on the Death Star. In the Death Star missions, the player assumes the role of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].

====Missions====
#[[Capture of the Sidral II|Guard Weapons Transfer]]
#[[Attack on the Imperial repair dock|Destroy Repair Dock]]
#[[Battle of the Corellian system|Capture Military Transport]]

#[[Capture of the Death Star design team|Intercept and Capture]]

#[[Attack on the Independence|Defend the ''Independence'']]
#Begin [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Assault]]
#[[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Attack Death Star Surface]]
#Death Star [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Trench Run]]

===Historical Missions: X-wing===

#[[Skirmish above Tatooine|Wingmen Are Important]]
#[[Raid above Coruscant|Destruction of Surveillance Satellites]]

===Historical Missions: Y-wing===

#[[Attack on TIE prototypes|Y-Wing Gunnery Practice]]
#[[Defense of the Karinne|S.O.S. from Corvette ''Karinne'']]
#[[Rescue mission to Kessel|Prisoners from Kessel]]
#[[Rescue mission to Mytus VII|Escape From Mytus VII]]

===Historical Missions: A-wing===
#[[Raid on an Imperial Repair Dock]]
#Aggressor Squadron Training
#[[Attack on TIE bombers|Intercept TIE Bomber Run]]
#[[Raid above Ghorman|Attack Run on Supply Freighters]]

#[[Reconnaissance of cargo containers|Deep Space Reconnaissance]]

===''Tour of Duty IV: Imperial Pursuit''===
Expansion 1, ''Imperial Pursuit'': The evacuation of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] and the search for a new secret location for a new base. The Rebels must resist growing Imperial pressure and the new [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor Cruiser]].
{{Main|Star Wars: X-Wing Tour of Duty: Imperial Pursuit}}

===''Tour of Duty V: B-Wing''===
Expansion 2, ''B-Wing'': Production of a new craft, the [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighter]], and its delivery to the Rebellion. The tour ends with the arrival on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] and the construction of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], setting the stage for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']].
{{Main|Star Wars: X-Wing Tour of Duty: B-Wing}}

===''X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM''===
Bonus missions included in this edition included historical missions, as well as "new" missions.

{{Main|Star Wars: X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM}}

==Gameplay==
''X-Wing'' operated on a custom-built graphics engine that used 3D polygons instead of the bitmaps used by the popular ''[[Wikipedia:Wing Commander (franchise)|Wing Commander]]'' games. Totally Games based the engine on their earlier [[Wikipedia:World War II|World War II]] flight simulators.

[[File:Xwingcockpit.png|thumb|left|Screenshot taken just before the standard end of the game]]
The player must complete missions ranging from simple dogfights with Imperial starfighters, through escort or capture for freighters or [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] to attacks on larger opposition ships. Each series of missions is organized linearly, having the player choose either death/capture or a retry at each failure.

Scoring advanced the player's [[Rebel command insignia/Legends|rank]] (Flight Cadet, [[Flight officer/Legends|Flight officer]], [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]], [[Commander/Legends|Commander]], [[General/Legends|General]]). He then could use his old pilot files and assign them as wingmen; this would improve their AI performance according to the rank of the pilot assigned to each wingman. This feature however was removed from the Win95 remakes as well as the Macintosh version.

Much of the game's challenge had to do with managing power resources ([[Laser/Legends|lasers]], [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] and engines), commanding wingmen, and using weapons effectively.

[[File:Independence2.jpg|thumb|right|Screenshot from the remade version]]
By combining the exciting space combat with cut scene story elements and the interactive [[iMUSE]] music system, ''X-Wing'' created an environment that recreated the atmosphere of the [[original trilogy]]'s space battles.

The game was split into three tours of duty which followed on from each other, though the player was free to tackle them in any order. The first two tours had twelve missions each, while the third had fourteen missions. Echoing the Alliance's hit-and-fade strategy, the majority of missions are concerned with protection, salvage, intelligence gathering and ambushing Imperial capital ships where feasible.

Apart from the tour of duty missions, players could explore the [[Pilot proving grounds]] to train and augment their scores on each of the three available ships, X-wing, Y-wing and A-wing; they can also fly 18 "historical missions" at the [[Rebel Combat Training Facilities]] for each ship, before beginning the standard scenarios. They could also examine ship schematics and review mission films that can be recorded in the memory cache during the missions, and afterwards saved to disk. There was also a Tech Room that displayed schematics of the ships in the game, naming many of the ships' systems and the companies that manufactured them.

The cutscenes were usually composed by screenshots taken from instances of the movies but combined to make a new narrative.

==Release and reception==
[[File:ImperialPursuit.jpg|thumb|right|180px|''Imperial Pursuit'', a ''Star Wars: X-Wing'' expansion pack]]

===Original release===
''X-Wing'' was developed for LucasArts by the team which would later become [[Totally Games]] with the story created by [[Lawrence Holland]], [[Edward Kilham]], [[David Wessman]] and [[David Maxwell]]. The original ''X-Wing'' game was released on floppy disk for PCs running DOS in 1993.

A limited edition version was made available with special packaging and a book titled ''[[The Farlander Papers]]'', which gave the background story of [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], the player's character, who also featured in the [[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] for the game.

===Expansion packs===
Two expansion packs, [[Star Wars: X-Wing Tour of Duty: Imperial Pursuit|''Imperial Pursuit'']] and [[Star Wars: X-Wing Tour of Duty: B-Wing|''B-Wing'']] were released shortly after. Each provided a tour of duty with an additional twenty missions while ''B-Wing'' also gave access to its namesake, the B-wing starfighter and another six historical missions for that ship. Two of these missions were remade versions of the tour of duty Death Star missions, allowing the player to fly the B-wing instead of an X-wing in the attack against the battlestation.

===The Collector Editions===
A year later (1994) ''X-Wing'' was re-released as ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM]]'', with the expansion packs included. It tweaks various areas of the game by including bug fixes, easy versions of some old missions, improved graphics, rehashed cutscenes, and bonus missions, as well as voiceovers for the mission briefings (unlike the in-game radio messages). The in-flight engine is improved to the caliber of the later [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] game, which is in turn an improved version of the original X-Wing game featuring Gouraud shading.

''X-Wing'' had a major retouch for its release along with the collections ''[[X-Wing Collector Series]]'' ([[1998]]) and ''X-Wing Trilogy'' ([[1999]]). The game was retrofitted with the [[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter|''X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter'']] graphics engine, which uses texture mapping instead of Gouraud shading. Concourse graphics and some cutscenes were also retouched and the game was now Windows 9x compatible.

[[File:XWing-GOG.png|thumb|right|275px|''Star Wars: X-Wing'' GOG.com re-release logo]]

===Re-release on GOG.com===
{{Quote|We are very excited to be able to bring these classic interactive titles to our platform; it has been a long-time dream of ours. Luckily, Disney Interactive is always looking for new ways to give players access to content, so this was a natural partnership. To those gamers playing these titles for the first time…May the Force Be With You!|Guillaume Rambourg, GOG.com managing director|<ref name="GOG.com Announcement">{{SW|url=news/x-wing-and-tie-fighter-return-on-gog-com|text=''X-Wing'' and ''TIE Fighter'' Return on GOG.com}}</ref>}}
On [[October 28]], [[2014]], [[Disney Interactive]] announced a new partnership with GOG.com, a DRM-free digital distributor, that would allow for more than twenty classic Lucasfilm video game titles to be re-released digitally for modern computers. Along with [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] and ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'',<ref name="GOG.com Announcement" /> ''Star Wars: X-Wing'' became available for digital download for a price of $9.99.<ref name="GOG.com" />

==Continuity==
===Inconsistencies===
Being a first ''Star Wars'' game with a major storyline, ''X-Wing'' began the game-contributed [[Canon#Canon in the Holocron continuity database|C-canon]]. As such, a number of contradictions to other sources appear that have required [[Retroactive continuity|retconning]] or other explanations.
[[File:ExecutorXwing.jpg|thumb|200px|The ''Executor'' in an in-game cutscene]]
*The abbreviated name of the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] in the game is "STD", unlike all later games of the series, in which the abbreviation "ISD" is used instead, following the introduction of the [[Interdictor-class cruiser|''Interdictor''-class cruiser]] (INT), [[Victory II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory II''-class Star Destroyer]] (VSD), and [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] (SSD).
*The game takes place before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], yet it features elements that canon establishes were created after the battle, such as the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari cruisers]], [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]], and [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]] which frequently appear in missions. The A-wing inconsistency was later resolved with the [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearhead]] retcon. The existence of the Interceptors was supported by [[West End Games]]' ''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]'' stating that at the time of Yavin, standard [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyers]] carried (among others) one squadron of Interceptors (12 craft).
**Another inconsistent appearance is that of the fully functional [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] appearing in a cutscene if the player is captured. However this cutscene is non-canon.
*The [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] homeworld is called ''Twi'lek'' instead of [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]].
*The game causes some confusion by showing the ''historical battles'' simulations as actual re-creations in space; it doesn't show the pilot entering a simulator, but leaving the mothership. It's never clear whether the [[pilot proving grounds]] are also simulation or actual flying in Rebel space. Briefings seem to be contradictory.
*Although based on the game alone, it seems that the player character ([[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]]) destroys the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] instead of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], ''The Farlander Papers'' (included with the strategy guide) explained that in the final mission, the player actually controls Luke Skywalker flying in Farlander's borrowed ship as [[Red Five/Legends|Red Five]]. Farlander flew with the [[Gold Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Gold Squadron]] in the battle.
*In the game, [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] are slower than [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]] but more damaging. However, many canon sources show that both proton torpedoes and concussion missiles are general types of weapons with various yields.

===Easter eggs===
If the player runs the game with their BIOS clock set to December 25, a [[Santa Claus/Legends|Santa Claus]] figure will appear in the [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] concourse.

At least in the 1998 Collector's Edition of ''X-Wing'', the first time the player visits the [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']] concourse after game installation, an animated figure resembling Marilyn Monroe can be seen standing near one of the hangar doors, in her iconic "flying skirt" pose.

Many names of ships, especially [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]], are English words or names [[Tuckerization|spelled backwards]] or are just anagrams. The most obvious examples are ''[[Nigiro]]'', ''[[Arreis]]'', ''[[Orcim]]/[[Esorp]]'', and [[Diputs group|''Diputs'']] (Origin, Microprose, and Sierra were all prominent software companies in the early '90s, while "Diputs" is a reversed spelling of "Stupid"). This tactic has been continued in the sequels, but to a lesser extent.

Some capital ships like ''[[Halifax]]'', ''[[Fugazi]]'', and [[The Tone|''Tone'']] have names referring to real names of ships or slang (usually Japanese of World War II or other real periods) of war.

Some ship names are homages to existing characters, such as the space transport group [[Renhoek (transports)|''Renhoek'']] and the Mon Calamari cruiser ''[[Stimsenj'kat]]'' in the first bonus mission of the ''X-Wing Collector's CD-ROM''. These refer to the main characters Ren Höek and Stimpson J. Cat from the cartoon [[Wikipedia:The Ren & Stimpy Show|''The Ren & Stimpy Show'']].

Other names and locations were borrowed from ''[[Han Solo at Stars' End]]''.
